seo_title: How Underwater Human-Robot Teaming Can Improve Maritime Missions
meta_description: Researchers at MIT Lincoln Laboratory are developing hardware and algorithms to improve collaboration between divers and autonomous underwater vehicles for…
body_html:
Underwater human-robot teaming is a growing area of research that aims to improve collaboration between divers and autonomous underwater vehicles.

Key Challenges in Underwater Human-Robot Teaming

Divers and AUVs generally don't team at all underwater due to the limitations of human dexterity and robot capabilities. Humans excel at recognizing objects underwater, but struggle with complex computations and high-speed mobility. ROVs are challenging to work with underwater in skilled manipulation tasks due to their limited agility.

Benefits of Underwater Human-Robot Teaming

Navigation and Perception Divers may use a compass and fin-kick counts to guide them, but robots can provide more accurate navigation and perception capabilities.

Manipulation Tasks Humans excel at recognizing objects underwater, while robots have an edge in processing power and endurance for complex computations and high-speed mobility.

Future Directions for Underwater Human-Robot Teaming Madeline Miller, principal investigator of the MIT Lincoln Laboratory project, notes that the team is developing hardware and algorithms to optimize maritime missions for the U.S. military.

source.title: Human-machine teaming dives underwater
source.summary: Researchers are developing hardware and algorithms to improve collaboration between divers and autonomous underwater vehicles engaged in maritime missions.
